name: Changhua County (Taiwan)

colors.symbolism: the fertile agricultural plain of central western Taiwan

description: A white field charged at the centre with a county emblem combining stylised Chinese characters and floral or wave-like forms in green and blue.
history: Changhua County (Zhanghua Xian) is a county on Taiwan's central west coast. Its current flag bearing the county emblem on a white field has been in use since the late 20th century. The emblem stylises the character 彰 (zhang, 'manifest', the first character in 彰化/Changhua) together with the agricultural and coastal motifs that define the county.

features.symbolism: stylised form of the character 彰 (zhang) from 彰化 (Changhua) combined with floral and wave-like forms representing the county's agricultural plain and coastal location
